title: Viperball Rules Evolution: Primer v1 vs Current Engine
scope: Audit of every area where the simulation engine has evolved beyond what the Primer describes. Items grouped by significance: structural changes that alter how the game plays, mechanical additions the Primer doesn't mention, and numerical recalibrations.

validation_claims.claim: Per AAR batch sim data: current average ~45-50 points per team, TDs per team ~1.9-3.5, snap kicks made ~3-5 per team, FGs per team ~3
validation_claims.check_method: Run batch sim and compare output metrics to claimed ranges

non_actions.reason: Not in Primer either; RULES.md has Overtime Rules section but engine has no overtime implementation. Games that end tied stay tied.
Engine uses 1-99 yard line system but doesn't model field width or end zone depth explicitly.

residual_risks: Scoring gravity tilted toward kicking plays rather than touchdowns; Primer's 4-6 TD target is aspirational
Run play base yards lower than Primer implies
section_headers_original: Viperball Rules Evolution: Primer v1 vs Current Engine
3. Penalties Use Power Plays, Not Yardage
15. Coaching Affects 8 Game Engine Decision Points
24. Pindown — Receiving Team Starts at 25, Not 20
26. Run Play Base Yards Differ from Primer
MISSING FROM ENGINE (In Primer But Not Implemented)
SUMMARY: What the Primer Gets Right vs. What's Moved
unmapped_content: This is a comparative audit/gap analysis document, not a standard AAR. It contains no scope/rationale/delegation/risk_posture in the AAR sense. Audit date: 2026-02-22. Source of truth: engine/game_engine.py (357KB), engine/coaching.py, engine/weather.py, engine/injuries.py, engine/epa.py. Compared against: Narrative Primer document (Feb 2026 snapshot). Full content: all 29 numbered sections comparing Primer vs engine including structural changes (quarter length, 4th down, penalties as power plays, lateral interceptions, timeouts), V2 engine architecture (Halo Model, Power Ratio, R/E/C archetypes, Star system, Hero Ball, Composure, bimodal yardage, prestige decisions), coaching system (4 staff, 15 staff slots, 6 attributes, 9 personality sliders, 20 hidden traits, contracts/marketplace), weather (11 climate zones, seasonal probability), injury model (5 tiers, 4 categories), VPA analytics, scoring values (all confirmed matching), and glossary of new terms (Kick Mode, Power Play, Halo, Tilt, Surge, VPA, Compelled Efficiency, R/E/C, Star Override, Hero Ball, Keeper Bell, DTD).
parser_notes: This document is a comparative engine audit / gap analysis, not a standard AAR. genre_confidence set to medium. It has the 'what was not done / what is missing from engine' shape (non_actions) and some residual risk acknowledgment, but no rationale, delegation events, or risk posture sections. Actions array is empty because the document describes the state of the engine, not what was done in a workstream. Date from document: 'Audited 2026-02-22 against engine commit history and live code.'
